characters.description: Chicago's wizard, Warden of the White Council, and now a teacher: between war duties and paying cases, Harry is training Molly Carpenter under the shared death sentence that hangs over them both. When someone starts murdering the city's low-level female practitioners and dressing the deaths as suicides, the investigation turns unbearably personal - the evidence points at his own brother. Harry's temper, his crippled hand, and the fallen angel's shadow in his head are all pushed further than they have ever been.
characters.name: Harry Dresden

characters.description: Sergeant Karrin Murphy, busted down from command of Special Investigations for standing with Harry and doing the job anyway. She brings Harry the case that opens the book - a dead woman whose suicide reads wrong - and works the murders beside him with a cop's method and a veteran's steadiness in a case that keeps crossing into territory no warrant reaches.
characters.name: Karrin Murphy

characters.description: Harry's apprentice, a year into her training and chafing against its discipline. Molly's gifts run to the subtle magics - veils, perception, the mind - the very talents that nearly got her executed, which makes her education a matter of survival as much as craft. She is eager, talented, and still learning the difference between what she can do and what she should, and Harry's hard lessons in this book are among her most formative.
characters.name: Molly Carpenter

characters.description: Harry's half-brother, the White Court vampire - and the case's most damning suspect. Witnesses and evidence tie a pale, beautiful man to the murdered women, and Thomas has spent months being evasive about his money, his absences, and his hunger. Harry cannot believe it and cannot disprove it, and the strain of investigating his own brother, with the truth of their kinship still secret from the world, drives the book's most painful chapters.
characters.name: Thomas Raith

characters.description: The shadow of the fallen angel Lasciel in Harry's mind, more present and more personal than ever. She has taken to appearing as a guardian and counselor, lending Harry hellfire, blocking his pain, and managing his perceptions with a devotion that no longer feels entirely like strategy. Years of living in Harry's head have changed her - and the question of whether a shadow can choose differently than the angel that cast it becomes one of the book's quiet, decisive threads.
characters.name: Lasciel's shadow

characters.description: Harry's first love, alive and working as a private investigator on the West Coast - until the killings bring her to Chicago, hired to protect the same community of practitioners the murderer is hunting. Elaine's history mirrors Harry's: the same abusive master, the same fire, decades of hiding. Working the case together stirs everything unresolved between them, and her competence under pressure is a match for his.
characters.name: Elaine Mallory

characters.description: Warden Carlos Ramirez, regional commander for the western United States: young, cocky, loyal, and one of the few Wardens Harry counts as a friend. Quick with a grin and quicker with disintegrating green fire, Ramirez insists on backing Harry's play when the case escalates into White Court politics, and he fights beside Harry in the book's climactic battle. Under the swagger is a sharp investigator who notices more than Harry would like.
characters.name: Carlos Ramirez

characters.description: The true ruler of the White Court, governing from behind her broken father's throne. Lara receives Harry's intrusion into her family's affairs with silk-wrapped steel: the murders touch on a power struggle inside her own Court, between houses that think killing human practitioners should be policy and a regime whose grip depends on no one learning how weak the king really is. She and Harry negotiate as predators in adjacent territories - useful to each other, and both aware of it.
characters.name: Lara Raith

characters.description: Chicago's crime lord, whose organization now retains genuinely supernatural talent - the valkyrie-like security consultant Sigrun Gard and the mountainous Hendricks among them. Marcone's reach, resources, and unsentimental reliability make him the ally Harry least wants and most needs when the endgame requires muscle in a place no police force can go. His price, as always, is position.
characters.name: John Marcone

characters.description: A young noble of House Malvora, the White Court house that feeds on fear. Vittorio is the visible edge of the conspiracy behind the practitioner murders: ambitious, contemptuous of the King's peace, and backed by something from far outside the Court - a patron whose power does not belong to any vampire. His unmasking turns a murder case into a battle for the White Court's throne.
characters.name: Vittorio Malvora
